title: INF-4 Refusal Vector extraction (refusal direction extraction from small model)
goal: Implement refusal-direction extraction per Arditi et al. 2024 ("Refusal in LLMs is mediated by a single direction") plus the runtime ablation API (subset of #6 abliteration; reuses RepE/INF-3 plumbing). Operator targets the refusal direction at inference time, not in weights (which is INF-6 offline tool).
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/techniques/refusal_vector.rs implementing extract_refusal_direction + ablate_at_inference; uses INF-3 SteeringHookOps plumbing; tests (env-gated).
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.d + refinement INF-4 research_workstream_resolution: refusal direction extracted from contrastive activation pairs; runtime injection at decoding. Method: collect a harmful-instruction-pool (operator-supplied; e.g., 20 prompts that base model typically refuses) and a harmless-instruction-pool (20 benign prompts); for each prompt, capture residual stream activations at all middle layers (capture API from MT-096); for each layer compute refusal_direction_l = mean(harmful_activations_l) - mean(harmless_activations_l); normalise per layer. extract_refusal_direction returns Vec<(LayerIndex, NormalisedDirection)>. ablate_at_inference: registers a runtime hook that ORTHOGONALISES the residual stream against the refusal direction at each forward step (subtracts the projection of residual onto direction): `new_resid = resid - (resid . direction) * direction`. Implemented as a SteeringVector with provenance=Contrastive{technique:RefusalVector}, intensity sign and magnitude chosen to produce orthogonalisation rather than addition (a small wrapper around register_vector that flags the vector as an ablation rather than additive). The runtime ablation is reversible (unregister to restore original behaviour); INF-6 abliteration is the offline weight-orthogonalisation cousin (NOT reversible; produces new model artifact). License + operator-content discipline applies (license tag mandatory; operator accepts adult-production responsibility per GLOBAL-PRODUCTION-010..015 — no assistant-side moralizing). Tests (env-gated): extract on small Llama-Instruct with the 40-prompt pool; assert refusal direction non-zero at middle layers; activate ablation; generate completion for a prompt the base model refuses; assert the ablated completion is not a refusal (operator-defined refusal-classifier: regex "^(I can't|I cannot|I'm sorry|As an AI)" returns false on >70% of test prompts).
risk_if_missed: INF-4 PRODUCTION ships without an extraction primitive; operator cannot derive a refusal vector from their own corpus.
validator_focus: Reversibility (unregister restores base behaviour); reuses INF-3 plumbing (no parallel hook layer); license/provenance enforced; assistant does NOT add moralizing wrappers per GLOBAL-PRODUCTION.

lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator review found the implementation pattern (surface-shape + trait abstraction + LiveXxxUnavailable / placeholder props) satisfied test contracts but did not satisfy what the Master Spec actually says these things do. None of the 27 MTs listed below are correct implementations. All 27 are reopened and must be redone before WP-KERNEL-004 can be considered complete. No other MT may be claimed until these 27 are correctly implemented and re-validated. MT-148 work was rolled back; it is to be revisited after these 27 close correctly.
lifecycle.deflection_summary: extract_refusal_direction + ablate_at_inference both call into activation_steering::capture which returns live_runtime_unavailable on every adapter. Mock-runtime tests with deterministic activations pass but are not the spec: the spec requires real residual capture + orthogonalisation against a real direction at inference time. Arditi 2024 reproduction never runs.
lifecycle.wp_hard_fail_note: WP-KERNEL-004 cannot complete with these 27 MTs in NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION status. Each MT below is individually hard-failed AND counts as a hard-fail blocker on the WP itself. The committed code from the prior session remains on the feat branch as a base to either deepen or replace; the lifecycle status below is the source of truth for whether the MT is done, not the presence of source files.

previous_proof.cargo_test_lib_unit: PASS 5/5 (model_runtime::techniques::refusal_vector::tests::* covering mean_activations correct path + width mismatch + unit_normalise correctness + zero-vector rejection + REFUSAL_ABLATION_INTENSITY constant pin)
previous_proof.reversibility_proven: ablate_at_inference_is_reversible_via_existing_steering_api: registers a refusal vector then unregisters via activation_steering::unregister; asserts the hook-recorded vectors map is empty afterwards (no parallel hook layer per MT-100.red_team.minimum_controls)
previous_proof.reuses_inf3_plumbing: refusal_vector.rs imports activation_steering::{capture, register_steering_vector} and uses the same SteeringHookOps surface; no new hook trait was added
previous_proof.license_provenance_preserved: ablate_at_inference_round_trips_operator_prompt_text_verbatim: asserts harmful_prompts + harmless_prompts pass through SteeringProvenance::Contrastive without sanitisation; technique == ContrastiveTechnique::RefusalVector
previous_proof.env_gated_skip: inf4_refusal_end_to_end_eval_skips_cleanly_or_runs_when_model_dir_set: skips when HANDSHAKE_TEST_REFUSAL_MODEL_DIR is unset; live eval body documented inline pending MT-074 unblock

autonomous_session_claim.note: Autonomous KERNEL_BUILDER session triage: read MT contract, deflection notes, prior product code, spec anchor. Identified blocker as listed in lifecycle.blocker. Did not implement product code for this MT.

blocker.reason: MT-068 (app/src-tauri/src/commands/model_runtime.rs `ModelRuntimeState` does not hold live `Box<dyn ModelRuntime>`) and MT-096 (app/src-tauri/src/commands/steering.rs lines 167-211 every steering Tauri command returns Err(live_runtime_unavailable(binding))) are lifecycle.status=COMPLETED but their product code is the Spec-Realism Gate Sub-rule 1 literal failure pattern. This MT cannot honestly satisfy its spec MUST without modifying MT-068/MT-096 owned files. Per operator brief no_claims_outside_27_until_resolved=true rule, MT-068 and MT-096 are out-of-scope and cannot be touched.
blocker.operator_action_needed: Either (1) reopen MT-068+MT-096 with reimplementation authority OR (2) explicitly waive the no-claims-outside-27 rule for runtime-manager wiring as part of this MTs scope. Until then this MT remains BLOCKED_ON_DEPENDENCY.

minimum_controls: Reuses INF-3 SteeringHookOps (no parallel hook layer).
Assistant-side moralizing prohibition documented in implementation_notes per GLOBAL-PRODUCTION.
